episodes.title: DAVID — Why Latin Never Really Died

brand.lighting: Warm brass desk lamp 3200K key — amber pool, no magenta, no cool fill

plates.host_reuse: host reference @2 — image_to_video frame 1 every DAVID host shot
plates.set_reuse: environment plate @1 — same set every episode

title_zone.youtube_safe: left 58% bottom 38% — face stays right; title never overlaps host eyes

host_crop.notes: Chest-up from locked host plate; vignette left edge into title zone

set_plate.blend: darken 65% under host crop for depth

copy.excerpt: Gallia est omnis divisa in partes tres

attestation.note: Liturgy & law continuous; classical vowel length from meter

episodes.imagine_prompt: YouTube thumbnail 1280×720, cinematic documentary prestige, high contrast readable at small size. LEFT THIRD: bold title-safe negative space for text overlay — gold hook title "LATIN NEVER DIED" and subtitle "Who kept speaking it?". RIGHT TWO-THIRDS: DAVID — synthetic keeper-of-the-Archive host, chest-up medium shot seated at the Archive worktable, 16:9. Male, reads age 45–55, dark hair flecked with silver neatly kept, lined intelligent face, calm attentive eyes. Charcoal fine-texture sweater, deep navy undertones, reading glasses pushed up into his hair, subtle worn leather strap detail on shoulder. Modern understated wardrobe NOT period costume. Still grounded presence, hands resting near open codex. Warm brass lamp key light, manuscript shelves soft behind. Documentary gravitas, trustworthy linguist host, no real person likeness, no celebrity resemblance, invented face only. Background depth from The Archive — a deep scholarly study and library interior, 16:9 cinematic wide shot, no people. Warm pools of amber lamplight against deep shadow. Floor-to-ceiling shelves hold manuscripts, scrolls, and clay tablets from many cultures receding into soft blur. A long wooden worktable in the foreground holds an open illuminated codex, a cuneiform clay tablet, a rolled papyrus scroll, and one recurring brass desk lamp with a soft glow. Documentary prestige aesthetic, rich wood and parchment textures, instant-recognition recurring TV host desk energy but ancient-archive themed, photoreal cinematic lighting. Foreground artifact prop: illuminated codex + Roman grammarian marginalia. Excerpt strip in Latin script: "Gallia est omnis divisa in partes tres". On-screen badges: ATTESTED + RECONSTRUCTED PRONUNCIATION. Brand wordmark top-left: DAVID · The Archive. COLOR LOCK Archive: amber 3200K brass lamp, burnt umber wood, parchment cream, warm shadow — no blue fill, no magenta, no teal. Synthetic host David-001 only, no real person likeness, no celebrity resemblance, SFW.
